title: Citrix Session Hijack Detection (Impossible Travel)
app: Citrix broker and gateway logs, MaxMind or internal geo lookup in Splunk, optional Splunk User Behavior Analytics
spl:
index=xd (sourcetype="citrix:broker:session" OR sourcetype="citrix:netscaler:aaalog") earliest=-24h
| iplocation client_ip
| eval country=coalesce(Country, geo_country, "unknown")
| sort 0 user, _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f first(_time) as prev_t first(client_ip) as prev_ip first(country) as prev_c by user
| eval km_s=if(isnotnull(prev_t) and (_time-prev_t)<3600 and prev_c!=country and country!="unknown" and prev_c!="unknown",1,0)
| where km_s=1
| table _time, user, prev_ip, client_ip, prev_c, country
description: Credential theft or session token reuse can show up as the same account active from two distant geographies within a short window, or as a client IP change mid-session without a clean re-authentication. Joining session and gateway events with geo data flags impossible travel and concurrent locations that warrant step-up auth and session termination.
value: Credential theft or session token reuse can show up as the same account active from two distant geographies within a short window, or as a client IP change mid-session without a clean re-authentication. Joining session and gateway events with geo data flags impossible travel and concurrent locations that warrant step-up auth and session termination.
implementation: Keep the geo lookup current. Use `streamstats` only as a starting point; refine with distance or travel-time models for fewer false positives from VPN exit changes. Pair with identity provider sign-in history. Auto-isolate high-value accounts. Document corporate VPN that shows a single country even when users are mobile.
visualization: Notable list: user, previous vs new country; map: client IP points; timeline: session events per user.

references.title: Citrix — Secure logon and session management

knownFalsePositives: VPN home regions, fast executive travel, mobile NAT churn, and shared egress IPs can trigger impossible travel without compromise. Add MFA success, new device, and PAM context; or require velocity plus second-factor challenge failure before an account takeover call.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• `index=xd`; `citrix:broker:session` and `citrix:netscaler:aaalog` with `user`, `client_ip`, `_time` (same TZ). `user` as UPN. Geo: monthly MaxMind (or iplocation + DB refresh). Stanzas: `props` so `client_ip` is always extracted.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
`lookups/jumpbox_subnets.csv` (allow), `known_vpn_exit_country.csv` (tune expectations). In `props` normalize nested IP fields. Drop duplicate session heartbeats: `dedup user,_time,client_ip` if needed in a subsearch.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
`streamstats` as starter: alert on any `km_s=1` to SecOps (critical). Add macro later for haversine distance. Combine with IdP travel alert in SOAR; winner = higher confidence. Service accounts: lookup exclude.

Step 3 — Validate
Backtest: known incident week, measure TP/FP. VPN flips: expect FP—tighten `(_time-prev_t)` or require both legs outside VPN allow list.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Runbook: step-up at IdP, kill Citrix session, reset creds for VIP. L3 for persistence. Document split-tunnel. Quarterly: tune time window 30–60m. Privacy: need-to-know for IP lists.
grandmaExplanation: We notice when the same sign-in shows up in two far-apart places faster than a person could travel — so you catch stolen or shared sign-ins before they quietly keep using your systems.
